title: Even Healthcare to set up second hospital in Indiranagar, Bengaluru
summary: Facility to be completed by mid-2026 as part of city-wide hospital network plan
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Facility to be completed by mid-2026 as part of city-wide hospital network plan

Even Healthcare has announced plans to establish its second hospital in Indiranagar, with an estimated investment of ₹30–40 crore. The facility, currently under development, is expected to be completed by mid-2026.

The upcoming hospital follows the launch of the company’s first hospital in May 2025 on Racecourse Road in Bengaluru, marking its transition to a fully integrated managed-care provider. The Indiranagar expansion forms part of the company’s plan to build a distributed hospital network across the city.

The expansion aligns with the company’s plan to ensure access to healthcare within a 5 km radius across Bengaluru, enabling residents to access coordinated care closer to their location.

Commenting on the expansion, Mayank Banerjee, Co-founder, Even Healthcare, said, “Our mission at Even is to make healthcare seamless, coordinated, and easily accessible for every patient. That means not only integrating care across doctors, technology, and hospitals, but also ensuring that physical access to care is never a barrier. As we expand across Bengaluru, we are working toward a network where an Even hospital is always within a 5km radius, so people can access dependable healthcare close to home.”

Over the past year, the company reported a 50 per cent reduction in post-surgery hospital readmissions in early 2025. It also completed its first ESOP buyback in March. In May 2025, the company launched its first hospital in Bengaluru. By September 2025, it reported a 92 per cent online revenue retention rate.

In December 2025, the company performed its first robotic knee replacement within six months of launching the hospital. Data from a tracked cohort across its hospital and care network recorded zero unplanned 30-day readmissions across more than 350 surgeries and zero post-operative infections. The network also reported more than 5,000 OPD visits delivered through clinics and hospital teams, over 200 avoided hospitalisations through monitored at-home recovery pathways, and an average length of stay at least 40 per cent shorter than comparable settings.
